summary: Operate the network-safe corner of the Azure estate: tag hygiene on virtual networks, public IPs, network interfaces, load balancers, and DNS zones, plus DNS record TTL tuning. Security groups, firewall rules, routes, VPN/ExpressRoute, peerings, and private endpoints are reachability-affecting and are not covered here — an engineer curates those separately.

operations.title: Update virtual network tags
operations.summary: Set or update this virtual network's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on addressing or connectivity.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the virtual network. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, and has no effect on the address space, subnets, or anything attached to it.

params.name: vnet
params.label: Virtual network
params.help: The virtual network to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Update public IP tags
operations.summary: Set or update this public IP's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on the address or how it's used.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the public IP address. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, and has no effect on the allocated address or what it is attached to.

params.name: public_ip
params.label: Public IP address
params.help: The public IP address resource to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Update network interface tags
operations.summary: Set or update this network interface's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on IP configuration.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the network interface. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, and has no effect on its IP configuration or the virtual machine it is attached to.

params.name: network_interface
params.label: Network interface
params.help: The network interface to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Update load balancer tags
operations.summary: Set or update this load balancer's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on traffic or rules.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the load balancer. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, and has no effect on frontend IPs, backend pools, or load-balancing rules.

params.name: load_balancer
params.label: Load balancer
params.help: The load balancer to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Update DNS zone tags
operations.summary: Set or update this DNS zone's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on records or resolution.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the DNS zone. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, and has no effect on the zone's records or name resolution.

params.name: dns_zone
params.label: DNS zone
params.help: The DNS zone to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Update DNS A record tags
operations.summary: Set or update this A record's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on resolution.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the DNS A record. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, and has no effect on the record's value or resolution.

params.name: record
params.label: A record
params.help: The A record to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Change a DNS A record's TTL
operations.summary: Change how long resolvers cache this A record. Lowering it first makes a later value change take effect faster.
operations.description: Adjusts how long resolvers cache this A record's answer, in seconds. In-place and reversible; a common pre-cutover step is lowering the TTL ahead of a planned value change so the switch propagates faster.

params.name: record
params.label: A record
params.help: The A record whose TTL will change.

params.name: ttl
params.label: New TTL (seconds)
params.help: Cache lifetime in seconds. 300 (5 minutes) suits a record you may change again soon; 3600 (1 hour) or more suits a stable record.

operations.title: Update DNS CNAME record tags
operations.summary: Set or update this CNAME record's tags. Only the tags you list change; others are left alone. No effect on resolution.
operations.description: Sets or merges cost-allocation and ownership tags on the DNS CNAME record. Non-disruptive, takes effect immediately, and has no effect on the record's value or resolution.

params.name: record
params.label: CNAME record
params.help: The CNAME record to tag.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to set; existing keys not listed here are left untouched.

operations.title: Change a DNS CNAME record's TTL
operations.summary: Change how long resolvers cache this CNAME record. Lowering it first makes a later value change take effect faster.
operations.description: Adjusts how long resolvers cache this CNAME record's answer, in seconds. In-place and reversible; a common pre-cutover step is lowering the TTL ahead of a planned value change so the switch propagates faster.

params.name: record
params.label: CNAME record
params.help: The CNAME record whose TTL will change.

params.name: ttl
params.label: New TTL (seconds)
params.help: Cache lifetime in seconds. 300 (5 minutes) suits a record you may change again soon; 3600 (1 hour) or more suits a stable record.

operations.title: Provision a new virtual network
operations.summary: Request a new virtual network with its own address space; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Create a virtual network as a complete specification: name, resource group, region and address space. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: vnet_name
params.label: Virtual network name
params.help: 2-64 characters; letters, numbers, periods, underscores and hyphens. Names the virtual network in the generated draft.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group this virtual network is created in.

params.name: address_space
params.label: Address space

bounds.semantic: internal cidr only — RFC1918, no public range, no 0.0.0.0/0

params.help: e.g. 10.20.0.0/16 — internal only. Additional ranges can be added by an engineer afterward.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Virtual network name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this virtual network is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this virtual network.

decisions: Additional address ranges or custom DNS servers beyond the one submitted
Peering to an existing hub or other virtual networks

operations.title: Provision a new subnet
operations.summary: Request a new subnet inside an existing virtual network; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Create a subnet as a complete specification: name, parent virtual network and its own address prefix. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: subnet_name
params.label: Subnet name
params.help: 2-64 characters; letters, numbers, periods, underscores and hyphens. Names the subnet in the generated draft.

params.name: virtual_network
params.label: Virtual network
params.help: The virtual network this subnet belongs to. Its address space must contain the subnet's own address prefix.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group this subnet is created in — Azure requires this to match the picked virtual network's own resource group.

params.name: address_prefix
params.label: Address prefix

bounds.semantic: internal cidr only — RFC1918, no public range, no 0.0.0.0/0

params.help: e.g. 10.20.1.0/24 — internal only, and must fall inside the virtual network's address space.

decisions: Confirm the resource group matches the picked virtual network's own resource group — Azure requires a subnet to live alongside its parent virtual network
Route table, NAT gateway or service-endpoint attachment happens afterward
Attaching a network security group to this subnet happens afterward

operations.title: Provision a new public IP
operations.summary: Request a new public IP address; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Create a public IP address as a complete specification: name, resource group, region, allocation method and SKU. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: public_ip_name
params.label: Public IP name
params.help: 2-64 characters; letters, numbers, periods, underscores and hyphens. Names the address in the generated draft.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group this public IP is created in.

params.name: sku
params.label: SKU
params.help: Standard is the modern, recommended choice and the only SKU compatible with a Standard load balancer; Basic is being retired by Microsoft and stays only for pairing with existing legacy resources.

params.name: allocation_method
params.label: Allocation method
params.help: Always static — the address never changes once assigned, and it is the only allocation method a Standard SKU accepts.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Public IP name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this public IP is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this public IP address.

decisions: Attaching this address to a load balancer, NAT gateway or network interface happens afterward
A DNS name label, if the address needs a stable hostname

operations.title: Provision a new load balancer
operations.summary: Request a new public-facing load balancer with its first frontend; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it's applied.
operations.description: Create a load balancer for a new public-facing service, with its first frontend wired to an existing public IP — the same depth the target-group action offers for backends. Submitting routes the complete specification to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish.

params.name: lb_name
params.label: Load balancer name
params.help: 2-64 characters; letters, numbers, periods, underscores and hyphens. Names the load balancer in the generated draft.

params.name: resource_group
params.label: Resource group
params.help: The resource group this load balancer is created in.

params.name: sku
params.label: SKU
params.help: Standard is the modern, recommended choice and requires a Standard SKU public IP; Basic is being retired by Microsoft and stays only for pairing with existing legacy resources.

params.name: frontend_name
params.label: Frontend name
params.help: Names this frontend inside the load balancer; referenced when wiring up rules afterward.

params.name: frontend_public_ip
params.label: Frontend public IP
params.help: The public IP address this load balancer's frontend uses to receive traffic. Its SKU and region must match this load balancer's own.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Load balancer name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this load balancer is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this load balancer.

decisions: Confirm the picked public IP's SKU and region match this load balancer's own — Azure requires the pairing to align
Backend pools, health probes and load-balancing rules are wired up afterward
An internal (private) load balancer is a separate engineer-authored variant
